encouragement: Keep moving. Tiny proof beats perfect plans.
A weak resume gets stronger every time you ship.
The timer is pressure, not prophecy.
One good project can change the whole job board.
Your inbox is quiet. Your build does not have to be.
Debt is loud. Momentum can be louder.
Open a window. Make a move. The week will not wait.
A small win now is better than a perfect plan later.
The job board only respects evidence.
Today does not need to be heroic. It needs to be useful.
Your future self is begging for one shipped thing.
No one hires panic. Build signal.
The desktop is messy. So is life. Click anyway.
You are one good keyword away from a different match.
Do not stare at the timer. Spend it.
Every action should buy cash, signal, skill, or sanity.
There is still time to make the resume less invisible.
A recruiter cannot scan effort they cannot see.
Proof first. Confidence follows.
The run is not over. The next click matters.
low_money: Cash is getting thin. Work or side gigs buy oxygen.
The account is blinking red. Earn before the week rolls over.
Discover is watching. Do not let the card become the plan.
Rent is not a suggestion. Find money.
Low balance means fewer mistakes allowed.
Money pressure is rising. Do not end the week broke.
Side gig now, dream job later. Survive first.
A good application means nothing if the lights go out.
The bank is not your mentor.
Cash buys time. Time buys options.
You can hate the gig and still need the gig.
The account is fragile. Protect the run.
Earn before the week eats you.
high_burnout: Burnout is spiking. Recovery is strategy, not weakness.
You are not a machine. Prep better by staying functional.
A tired interview answer costs more than a rested pause.
The grind is starting to tax the run.
Do not brute-force your way into a worse interview.
Rest is not glamorous. Neither is failing from exhaustion.
Your brain is part of the build system.
Burnout is a hidden debt with interest.
Slow down just enough to keep going.
A recovered player makes better choices.
The strongest move might be not collapsing.
interview: Interview unlocked. Prep once, breathe twice, then answer cleanly.
A recruiter is looking. Do not waste the opening.
This is the part where proof turns into a conversation.
The inbox made noise. That is not nothing.
You have a shot. Make it sound intentional.
Prep now. Panic later if you must.
The resume opened the door. The answers keep it open.
Do not wing the only warm lead.
Practice one answer before you click in.
Interview ready means opportunity, not safety.
This is where projects become stories.
The recruiter wants signal. Give them signal.
score_gain: Score jumped. That is real signal.
Nice. The resume is getting less invisible.
That one counts. Ship, apply, repeat.
Coins up. The run has pulse.
Evidence added. Recruiters squint less now.
Good. Do that again, but smarter.
The board just got a little more forgiving.
The resume just got harder to ignore.
That was not busywork. That counted.
A real gain. Keep moving before the week steals it.
Numbers went up. Morale is allowed.
You bought yourself a better chance.
week: New week. New bills. New chance.
Rent took its bite. Time to bite back.
Another week starts. The game is still winnable.
Fresh week. Same pressure. Better choices.
The week reset. Your problems did not.
Bills landed. Now answer with action.
A new week is not mercy. It is a timer.
The calendar is sprinting. You should too.
Every week needs a plan: cash, signal, applications.
The run continues. Make this week count.
projects: Project work turns effort into evidence. Open Projects before applying cold.
One shipped project can unlock the keyword the job board is begging for.
If every job says missing keywords, build the missing proof.
Recruiters cannot scan vibes. Ship something visible.
A project is not homework here. It is leverage.
Build before blasting apps if the match percentages look cursed.
Keywords are not decorations. They are doors.
Open Projects and make the resume less empty.
The job board rewards proof more than hope.
No completed projects yet? That is the cleanest next move.
A tiny shipped thing beats a giant unfinished idea.
Your resume needs artifacts, not apologies.
Pick a project that adds a keyword you keep seeing on jobs.
Build signal first. Applications get louder after that.
jobs: You have enough keywords to test the market. Open Job Board.
Do not wait for the perfect resume. Check matches now.
The board changes once your keywords grow. Re-open Jobs and scan.
If a card is close, one project may push it into Interview Ready.
Apply where the match is strong. Save panic applications for never.
Use the job board like a map, not a wishing well.
Good matches are hiding in plain sight. Open Jobs.
A keyword-rich resume only helps if you actually apply.
The job board is your thermometer. Check the temperature.
Strong match? Click before the week eats your action points.
If every match is weak, go back to Projects. If one is strong, apply.
Resume signal is up. Time to see who notices.
applications: Applications went out. Now improve the odds before the next batch.
Ghosting is data. Build the missing signal and try again.
If nobody bites, do not only send more. Send better.
Rejected? The resume is telling you what to build next.
A quiet inbox means adjust the strategy, not surrender.
Applications are lottery tickets until the match percentage improves.
Track what jobs keep asking for, then build that.
A warm match beats ten cold panic clicks.
Every rejection should point to a project, skill, or prep move.
The inbox is not the scoreboard. The scoreboard is the scoreboard.
Send apps, then increase signal. That loop is the game.
timer: Clock is low. Spend the action points or end the week deliberately.
The timer is almost gone. Choose cash, proof, prep, or close the week.
Do not let the week expire with useful moves still on the table.
Seconds are leaking. Make the next click count.
Low time means no wandering. Pick the most urgent window.
If action points are gone, end week before the clock bullies you.
Timer red means strategy now, not browsing.
The week is closing. Grab one gain if you can.
You can still turn a bad week into a useful one.
Clock pressure is part of the job search simulation. Breathe, then act.
confidence: Confidence is low. Prep can stabilize the next interview.
A little prep now can stop one bad answer from wrecking the run.
Confidence is not magic. It rises when you practice.
Open Prep if the interview screen feels scary.
Low confidence makes good opportunities harder to convert.
The player needs proof, but the player also needs nerve.
If you feel shaky, practice before clicking Interview.
Build confidence before the recruiter asks the obvious question.
Preparation is cheaper than fumbling a warm lead.
Confidence below half means Prep has real value.
debt: Debt is active. Watch cash before ending the week.
Discover minimum payments will quietly chew the run.
The card bought time, not freedom.
Open Discover if the money picture feels suspicious.
Debt pressure means side gig may be the strategic move.
Do not let a good job plan die to a bad balance.
When debt is loud, cash actions matter more.
Minimum payments are coming whether the resume is ready or not.
Debt does not end the run today, but it narrows tomorrow.
actions: No action points left. End Week is probably the clean move.
You spent the week. Check stats, then roll forward.
Action points are empty. The dock has the next button.
If nothing else can be done, end the week before the timer does it for you.
AP is gone. Read Today's Stats, then commit to the next week.
The week is spent. Let the consequences land.
No moves left means no more overthinking.
End Week is scary because it matters. Click it anyway when AP is empty.
You did what you could this week. Next week, choose faster.
Out of actions. Strategy shifts to review and survival.
